title: Sheep Among Wolves
subtitle: Christ sends out his disciples and arms them for patience in suffering

stillness: Be still before the One who sends you.
Let the power of Him who sends quiet your fear.
Before the danger, behold first the authority that goes with you.
gospel: as sheep in the midst of wolves.
for they will deliver you up.
behold: Behold the Lord, your Master, who chose you, who taught you, who armed you.
He sends you out not as the defenceless, but as the chosen, as instructed teachers, as armed soldiers, as skilled physicians.
Behold yourselves sent as sheep, as lambs — that is, simple and innocent ones, not defending yourselves by weapons.
You go to nourish others with the milk of teaching, to draw them by the example of a gentle way of life, and to lay down your very bodies for them.
Behold where you are sent: into the midst of the wolves, into the midst of the Scribes and Pharisees and the other cruel persecutors and slanderers,
who are called wolves for their rapacity, their cruelty, and their unlikeness.
See: He says in the midst of the wolves, that your patience may be as a centre to every injury surrounding it.
consider: As gentle ones you go among the cruel, to suffer; guard your patience and your innocence.
Do not defend yourselves, for you are sheep: let those rage who are wolves.
A wondrous hunter is the Lord, who by lambs, by sheep, captures and conquers the wolves; by patience He overcomes power.
Be wise as serpents against the cunning and deceit of the Scribes — to understand and avoid their frauds and snares.
And simple as doves against the cruelty and malice of the tyrants — to bear and to forgive the wounds and the wrongs.

As the serpent exposes the rest of its body to peril for the head, in which its life is, and guards the head with its whole body: so guard your Head, that is, me, and the faith, and the soul, whole and uncorrupted.
And as doves are without gall and without the bitterness of malice, and do harm to no one: so, guarding your innocence, do not inflict evil nor return it to others.
By prudence avoid evil, and by simplicity do evil to no one.
Beware of men, the worse wolves — beware of the persuasions of men, who seek by fraud to deceive you and by violence to pervert you.
For above all evils, man is the worst evil.
Compare him to the beast: you will find him worse. The beast, though cruel, is irrational, and so its cruelty may be turned aside; but a man's cruelty, since he is rational, is not easily escaped.
Compare him to the serpent: you will find him worse. The serpent has malice, yet it has the fear of man; if it can, it bites, and if it cannot, it flees.
But the man has the malice of the serpent, and has not the fear the serpent has.
So long as he has no occasion, he lies hidden like a serpent; but if he finds his occasion, he rushes in like a beast.
Today the Lord still sends his disciples as sheep among wolves; but alas, today many shepherds are among their flock as wolves among the sheep.
prayer: who sent your disciples to do battle against the world and the devil,
and gave them consolation in the bearing of tribulations:
and grant me firmness against the perils of the world and the snares of the devil.
Guard me from my enemies, visible and invisible,
and give me patience in adversities and tribulations,
that I may always know you, my Creator, to be my Defender,
and being always defended by you in this present life,
may come after it to you, absolved of all my sins. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Mirabilis venator est Dominus, qui per agnos, sive oves, capit et devincit lupos; per patientiam vincit potentiam.
latinJewel.english: A wondrous hunter is the Lord, who by lambs, by sheep, captures and conquers the wolves; by patience He overcomes power.
